title: Theme
description: A renderer-agnostic theme: a set of presentation CHOICES expressed purely as SEMANTIC TOKENS. Every token is a closed, enum-constrained vocabulary of meaning (e.g. `emphasis: none|low|high`, `spacing: compact|cosy|roomy`) — never a medium-specific value. A theme carries NO pixels, hex colours, fonts, CSS, or any HTML/medium detail; a renderer is free to map each semantic choice onto whatever concrete styling its medium uses. This schema is the shared theme vocabulary referenced by the document default theme (E2-S2) and a block wrapper's per-block theme override (E2-S3). Tokens are ordinary scalar fields drawn from the variable type set (each an `enum` over a fixed value list), so a theme is fully describable through the existing configurable-surface mechanism with no special chrome subsystem. The set is intentionally small and structured as a `base` group of cross-cutting tokens (`$defs/baseTokens`) that any future per-type token group can extend, so the vocabulary can grow without breaking existing themes.

baseTokens.title: Base Theme Tokens
baseTokens.description: The cross-cutting semantic tokens that apply uniformly to whatever a theme styles, independent of any particular item type. Each token is optional; an omitted token means "inherit / renderer default". This object is the extension point for the vocabulary: future per-type token groups compose alongside these base tokens rather than redefining them.

emphasis.title: Emphasis
emphasis.description: How prominent the styled element should appear relative to its surroundings. `none` recedes, `low` is quiet, `high` is foregrounded. Renderer-agnostic: maps to whatever weight/contrast/size cue a medium uses.

spacing.title: Spacing
spacing.description: The relative amount of internal/outer breathing room. `compact` is tight, `cosy` is balanced, `roomy` is generous. No absolute units — a renderer chooses concrete distances.

density.title: Density
density.description: How tightly repeated/listed content is packed. `comfortable` favours legibility, `compact` favours fitting more in view. Distinct from `spacing`, which governs surrounding room rather than per-item packing.

tone.title: Tone
tone.description: The semantic colour intent, expressed by MEANING rather than any colour value. `neutral` is the default, `accent` highlights, and `positive`/`caution`/`critical` carry status meaning. A renderer maps each to its own palette; no hex is implied.

radius.title: Corner Radius
radius.description: How softened the corners of the styled surface are. `none` is square, `subtle` is gently eased, `rounded` is pronounced. No pixel radius — a renderer picks concrete values.

border.title: Border Weight
border.description: How present a separating edge is around the styled surface. `none` omits it, `hairline` is the thinnest visible divider, `standard` is a normal edge. No widths or colours — a renderer decides.
